title: Bass Identification: Largemouth vs Smallmouth vs Spotted Bass
description: Learn to tell the difference between largemouth, smallmouth, and spotted bass. Key identification features for each species.
intro: Bass are the most popular game fish in North America, but telling the different species apart can be tricky — especially spotted bass and largemouth. Here's how to identify each species at a glance.

title: Best Bass Fishing Lakes in the United States by Region
description: The top bass fishing lakes across the United States, organized by region. From Florida to California, find the best bass waters for your next trip.
intro: The United States offers incredible bass fishing from coast to coast. Whether you target largemouth, smallmouth, or spotted bass, every region has world-class waters. Here are the top bass fishing lakes in the US, organized by region.

title: Best Fishing Apps for Canada and the United States in 2026
description: A review of the best fishing apps for anglers in Canada and the United States, including catch logging, fish identification, weather, maps, and more.
intro: There are dozens of fishing apps on the market, but only a few are truly designed for Canadian and US waters. Here's a comparison of the best fishing apps available in 2026.

title: Best Fishing Lakes in British Columbia for Salmon
description: Discover the top salmon fishing lakes and rivers in British Columbia, from the Fraser River to Haida Gwaii. Species, seasons, and tips for every angler.
intro: British Columbia is a world-class salmon fishing destination. From the mighty Fraser River to the pristine waters of Haida Gwaii, BC offers some of the best salmon fishing on the planet. Whether you're chasing Chinook, Coho, Sockeye, Pink, or Chum, here are the top locations every salmon angler should fish.

title: Best Fishing Rod and Reel Combos for Beginners
description: A guide to choosing the best fishing rod and reel combinations for beginners, covering spinning, baitcasting, and all-purpose setups for Canadian and US waters.
intro: Buying your first fishing rod and reel can be overwhelming. There are dozens of combinations, confusing terminology, and price ranges from $20 to $500+. Here's a straightforward guide to choosing the right setup for beginners.

title: Best Fishing Times: Solunar Tables Explained
description: Learn how moon phases, sunrise, and sunset affect fish feeding behaviour. Use solunar predictions to plan your most productive fishing trips across Canada and the US.
intro: Every experienced angler knows that timing matters. You can have the perfect lure, the right gear, and a spot that's loaded with fish — but if you're there at the wrong time, you might not get a single bite. That's where solunar tables come in.

title: Best Lures for Bass Fishing in Canada and the United States
description: A complete guide to the best lures for bass fishing in North America, including topwater, spinnerbaits, crankbaits, jigs, and soft plastics for every situation.
intro: Choosing the right lure can make the difference between a great day on the water and a frustrating one. Here's a guide to the most effective bass lures for waters across Canada and the United States.

title: Best Times to Fish for Every Species in North America
description: A seasonal guide to the best times to fish for popular species across Canada and the United States, including solunar periods, seasonal patterns, and daily windows.
intro: Knowing when to fish is half the battle. Different species have different peak feeding times, and those times change with seasons, weather, and location. Here's a comprehensive guide to the best fishing times for North America's most popular species.

title: Catch and Release Best Practices: A Complete Guide
description: A complete guide to responsible catch and release fishing. Learn the best techniques for handling, unhooking, and releasing fish to ensure their survival.
intro: Catch and release is one of the most important conservation practices available to anglers. When done correctly, it allows fish to survive and reproduce, ensuring healthy fisheries for future generations. Here's how to do it right.

title: Common Panfish of North America: Complete Identification Guide
description: Complete guide to identifying the most common panfish species in Canada and the United States, including sunfish, crappie, perch, and more.
intro: Panfish are the most accessible and popular group of fish for anglers of all ages across North America. This guide covers the most common species found in Canada and the United States, with key identification features for each.

title: Digital Fishing Log: Why You Should Track Your Catches
description: Discover the benefits of keeping a digital fishing log. Learn how tracking your catches helps you become a better angler and preserves your fishing memories.
intro: Every angler remembers their biggest fish. But what about the patterns? The lure that worked on that one specific lake? The exact date the walleye turned on last year? A digital fishing log captures all of this and more.

title: Fishing the Great Lakes: A Complete Species-by-Species Guide
description: A complete guide to fishing the Great Lakes — Lake Ontario, Erie, Huron, Michigan, and Superior. Species, locations, and techniques for each lake.
intro: The Great Lakes — Superior, Michigan, Huron, Erie, and Ontario — hold 20% of the world's freshwater and support some of the most diverse and productive fisheries in North America. Whether you're fishing from shore, a boat, or through the ice, each lake offers something unique.

title: Fishing in Alberta: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Alberta — from world-class trout on the Bow River to walleye, pike, and lake trout in pristine northern lakes.
intro: Alberta may be known for the Rocky Mountains and the Calgary Stampede, but its fishing is world-class. The province spans four distinct eco-regions — mountains, foothills, prairies, and boreal forest — each offering unique fishing opportunities. From brown trout and rainbows in the Bow River to walleye and lake trout in the north, here are the top species and where to find them.

title: Fishing in British Columbia: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in British Columbia — from salmon and halibut off the coast to steelhead, sturgeon, and trout in legendary rivers and lakes.
intro: British Columbia is a fishing paradise. The province spans everything from the Pacific Ocean to the Rocky Mountains, offering world-class saltwater and freshwater fishing. Whether you are after a 50-pound king salmon, a massive white sturgeon, or a pristine alpine trout, BC has it all. Here is a guide to the top species and where to find them.

title: Fishing in Florida: Top Species & Where to Find Them
description: A complete guide to fishing in Florida — the fishing capital of the United States. Species, locations, seasons, and tips for freshwater and saltwater fishing.
intro: Florida is unquestionably the fishing capital of the United States. With more than 7,700 lakes, 11,000 miles of rivers, and 2,276 miles of coastline, the variety of fishing opportunities is unmatched. Here's your guide to the top species and where to find them.

title: Fishing in Manitoba: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Manitoba — from Lake Winnipeg walleye and the Red River catfish run to trophy lake trout on the Canadian Shield.
intro: Manitoba sits at the crossroads of the prairies and the Canadian Shield, offering a remarkable diversity of fishing. The province's waterways range from the vast, windswept Lake Winnipeg to remote fly-in shield lakes. Manitoba is known internationally for its walleye (called pickerel locally) and channel catfish fisheries, but it also offers exceptional pike, lake trout, and smallmouth bass fishing.

title: Fishing in New Brunswick: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in New Brunswick — home of the legendary Miramichi River and world-class Atlantic salmon, brook trout, and smallmouth bass.
intro: New Brunswick is Atlantic salmon country. The province's legendary rivers — the Miramichi, Restigouche, and Nepisiguit — draw anglers from around the world. But New Brunswick also offers excellent brook trout fishing, outstanding smallmouth bass in the Saint John River system, and some of the best striped bass fishing on the East Coast.

title: Fishing in Newfoundland and Labrador: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Newfoundland and Labrador — from Atlantic salmon rivers and sea-run brook trout to Arctic char and trophy pike in Labrador.
intro: Newfoundland and Labrador is Canada's easternmost province, a land of rugged coastlines, boreal forests, and countless rivers and lakes. The province offers world-class Atlantic salmon fishing, exceptional sea-run brook trout, and remote wilderness fishing in Labrador that rivals anything on the continent. Newfoundland's freshwater fishing is supplemented by excellent saltwater opportunities for cod, halibut, and tuna.

title: Fishing in Northwest Territories: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Northwest Territories — home of Great Bear Lake's trophy lake trout and world-class Arctic grayling, pike, and Arctic char.
intro: The Northwest Territories offer some of the most spectacular freshwater fishing in the world. Great Bear Lake and Great Slave Lake are legendary destinations for trophy lake trout, while the territory's countless rivers and smaller lakes hold exceptional populations of Arctic grayling, northern pike, Arctic char, and inconnu.

title: Fishing in Nova Scotia: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Nova Scotia — from Atlantic salmon rivers and sea-run brook trout to smallmouth bass and striped bass.
intro: Nova Scotia is defined by the sea. The province's countless rivers and lakes offer exceptional freshwater fishing, while the surrounding Atlantic Ocean provides world-class saltwater opportunities. Nova Scotia is particularly famous for its Atlantic salmon rivers and sea-run brook trout, but the province also offers excellent smallmouth bass, striped bass, and trout fishing.

title: Fishing in Nunavut: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Nunavut — world-class Arctic char fishing in pristine tundra rivers, plus lake trout, Arctic grayling, and brook trout in Canada's far north.
intro: Nunavut is Canada's newest, largest, and most remote territory. Fishing here is not for everyone — it requires significant travel, expense, and preparation. But for those who make the journey, Nunavut offers some of the most pristine and productive fishing in the world. Arctic char is the star, but the territory also offers exceptional lake trout, Arctic grayling, and brook trout fishing.

title: Fishing in Ontario: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Ontario — from Great Lakes salmon and trout to world-class bass, walleye, and muskie in countless lakes and rivers.
intro: Ontario is a fishing giant. With four of the five Great Lakes, over 250,000 lakes, and countless rivers, the province offers an incredible diversity of fishing opportunities. From the warm-water bass fisheries of the south to the cold, clear trout lakes of the north, Ontario has something for every angler. This guide covers the top species and where to find them across the province.

title: Fishing in Prince Edward Island: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Prince Edward Island — from world-class brook trout streams and striped bass to bluefin tuna off the coast.
intro: Prince Edward Island may be Canada's smallest province, but its fishing is anything but small. PEI's gentle rivers and streams are renowned for their quality brook trout fishing, while the surrounding waters of the Gulf of St. Lawrence offer striped bass and some of the best bluefin tuna fishing in the world.

title: Fishing in Quebec: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Quebec — from Atlantic salmon rivers and St. Lawrence bass to remote northern brook trout and lake trout.
intro: Quebec is Canada's largest province by area, and its fishing opportunities are equally expansive. The province spans from the warm waters of the St. Lawrence River Valley to the Arctic tundra of Nunavik. Quebec is home to world-renowned Atlantic salmon rivers, exceptional brook trout fishing, and productive walleye, pike, and bass waters. Here are the top species and where to find them.

title: Fishing in Saskatchewan: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Saskatchewan — from walleye and pike in Tobin Lake and Lac La Ronge to trophy lake trout in the northern shield lakes.
intro: Saskatchewan is home to over 100,000 lakes and more fishable water than any other Canadian province. The Saskatchewan Record Fish Registry tracks over 40 species, and the province's northern waters produce trophy-sized fish year after year. From the fertile prairie reservoirs to the deep, clear shield lakes of the north, here are the top species and where to find them.

title: Fishing in Yukon: Top Species and Best Spots
description: Discover the best fishing in Yukon — from world's largest Chinook salmon in the Yukon River to Arctic grayling, lake trout, and northern pike in pristine wilderness.
intro: The Yukon is a wilderness fishing destination like no other. The territory's vast, pristine waters produce some of the largest Chinook salmon in the world, along with exceptional populations of Arctic grayling, lake trout, and northern pike. The short but intense summer fishing season offers anglers a chance to experience true northern wilderness.

title: Fishing Knots Every Angler Should Know (With Diagrams & Video)
description: Complete guide to 12 essential fishing knots with step-by-step SVG diagrams, video tutorials, line-type compatibility, and common mistakes to avoid.
intro: A fishing knot is the weakest link in your setup. A poorly tied knot can lose you a trophy fish, while a well-tied one gives you confidence that every cast might be the one. The good news? You only need a handful of knots to cover almost every fishing situation.

title: Fishing License Guide: Canada vs US Requirements
description: A complete guide to fishing license requirements across Canada and the United States. What you need, where to get it, and how much it costs.
intro: Before you cast a line, you need a fishing license. Requirements vary by province and state, and the rules can be confusing — especially if you're crossing borders. Here's a guide to fishing licenses across Canada and the United States.

title: Fishing Safety Tips: Stay Safe on the Water
description: Essential fishing safety tips — weather awareness, lightning safety, boat and kayak safety, hook removal, sun protection, and what to do in an emergency.
intro: Fishing is one of the safest outdoor activities you can enjoy. But like any time spent on or near water, things can go wrong if you're not prepared. A little knowledge and a few pieces of gear can make the difference between a great day on the water and a trip to the emergency room.

title: Fishing with Kids: A Complete Guide for Parents
description: A complete guide to fishing with kids — choosing gear, finding the right spot, keeping them engaged, safety tips, and making every trip fun.
intro: Some of the best memories an angler can have involve a child catching their first fish. But taking kids fishing can also be stressful if you're not prepared — short attention spans, tangles, hooks, and boredom can derail a trip fast. This guide will help you set up for success before you even leave the driveway.

title: Fishing with Voice Controls: How Hands-Free Logging Works
description: Learn how voice-controlled fishing apps let you log catches without touching your phone. Perfect for kayak fishing, boat fishing, or any hands-free situation.
intro: Voice control is changing the way anglers log their catches. Instead of fumbling with your phone while trying to land a fish, you can simply speak your catch details and keep fishing.

title: Freshwater Fish of the Great Lakes: Species Identification Guide
description: A complete guide to the freshwater fish species found in the Great Lakes, from salmon and trout to bass, walleye, and panfish.
intro: The Great Lakes — Superior, Michigan, Huron, Erie, and Ontario — hold about 20% of the world's surface freshwater and support one of the most diverse freshwater fish communities on Earth. Here's a guide to the key species you'll encounter.

title: How to Catch Northern Pike: Lures, Locations, Techniques
description: A complete guide to catching northern pike in Canada and the United States. Learn about pike behavior, best lures, top locations, and proven techniques.
intro: Northern pike are one of North America's most aggressive and exciting freshwater predators. With their long, torpedo-shaped bodies and mouthful of razor-sharp teeth, they're the wolves of the freshwater world. Here's how to catch them.

title: How to Catch Walleye: Tips for Beginners
description: A complete beginner's guide to catching walleye. Learn where to find them, what gear to use, and the best techniques for walleye fishing in Canada and the US.
intro: Walleye are one of the most popular game fish in North America, prized for their excellent table fare and challenging nature. If you're new to walleye fishing, here's everything you need to know to start catching them consistently.

title: How to Choose a Fishing Rod and Reel: Complete Guide
description: A comprehensive guide to selecting fishing rods and reels individually. Covers rod action, power, length, reel gear ratio, drag, and how to match them for any fishing situation in Canada and the US.

title: How to Choose the Right Fishing Line: Mono, Fluoro, Braid
description: A complete guide to choosing fishing line — monofilament, fluorocarbon, and braided line — for different fishing situations in Canada and the US.
intro: Choosing the right fishing line is one of the most important decisions you'll make as an angler. The wrong line can cost you fish; the right line makes everything easier. Here's a complete guide to the three main line types.

title: How to Identify Salmon Species: Chinook, Coho, Sockeye, Pink, Chum
description: Learn to identify the five Pacific salmon species found in North American waters. A visual guide to Chinook, Coho, Sockeye, Pink, and Chum salmon.
intro: Five species of Pacific salmon are found in North American waters — Chinook, Coho, Sockeye, Pink, and Chum. In the Great Lakes, Chinook and Coho have been successfully introduced. Here's how to identify each species.

title: How to Identify Trout Species: Rainbow, Brown, Brook, Cutthroat
description: Learn to identify the four most common trout species in Canada and the United States: rainbow, brown, brook, and cutthroat trout.
intro: Trout are among the most popular game fish in North America, but telling species apart can be tricky. Here's a simple guide to identifying the four most common trout species found across Canada and the United States.

title: How to Plan a Fishing Trip: Step-by-Step Guide
description: A complete guide to planning a successful fishing trip, from choosing a destination to packing gear and booking accommodations.
intro: A well-planned fishing trip is the difference between an amazing experience and a frustrating one. Whether you're planning a day trip to a local lake or a week-long adventure in a remote province, here's a step-by-step guide to planning your perfect fishing trip.

title: How to Read a Fish Finder: Sonar Basics for Anglers
description: A beginner's guide to reading fish finders and sonar. Learn to identify fish, structure, bottom composition, and water temperature on your fish finder screen.
intro: A fish finder is one of the most valuable tools an angler can own. But when you first turn one on, the screen can look like a confusing mess of lines, blobs, and numbers. Here's how to read a fish finder and actually understand what you're seeing.

title: Ice Fishing Gear & Techniques: Complete Guide
description: Complete guide to ice fishing gear and techniques — augers, shelters, rods, electronics, safety gear, and tips for targeting walleye, perch, pike, and trout through the ice.
intro: Ice fishing turns frozen lakes into a whole new fishery. It's a uniquely social, mobile, and technical form of fishing that's deeply rooted in Canadian and northern US culture. Whether you're targeting panfish through a hand-augered hole or chasing trophy pike from a heated shelter, ice fishing rewards preparation and knowledge.

title: Ice Fishing Hotspots in Canada: Top 10 Lakes
description: Discover the best ice fishing lakes in Canada for walleye, lake trout, perch, and pike. Locations, species, and tips for a successful ice fishing trip.
intro: Ice fishing is a Canadian tradition. From December through March, anglers across the country head onto frozen lakes in search of walleye, lake trout, perch, and pike. Here are the top ice fishing destinations in Canada.

title: How to Identify 5 Common Panfish in Canada & the US
description: Learn to identify bluegill, pumpkinseed, crappie, yellow perch, and warmouth. Simple tips to tell these popular panfish apart on any lake.
intro: Panfish are the most accessible freshwater fish in North America. They're the fish many of us caught as kids, and they remain a favourite for anglers of all skill levels. But with so many similar-looking species, telling them apart can be tricky. Here's a simple guide to the five most common panfish across Canada and the United States, with key identification tips for each.

title: Kayak Fishing Guide for Canada & the US
description: A complete guide to kayak fishing in Canada and the United States — choosing a kayak, essential gear, safety tips, top species, and best launch spots.
intro: Kayak fishing is one of the fastest-growing segments of the sport. It's quiet, affordable, and gets you into places power boats can't reach. Whether you're paddling a quiet lake in Ontario, working the shoreline of a bass reservoir in Texas, or chasing salmon along the BC coast, a kayak opens up a whole new way to fish.

title: Lake Trout Fishing in Canada's Northern Lakes
description: A guide to lake trout fishing in Canada's northern lakes and territories. Top locations, techniques, and tips for catching trophy lake trout.
intro: Lake trout are the undisputed kings of Canada's northern waters. These cold-water predators can live for 50+ years and grow to massive sizes — fish over 50 pounds have been caught in Canadian waters. Here's where and how to catch them.

title: Night Fishing: Tips, Species & Safety
description: Complete guide to night fishing — best species to target at night, essential gear, safety tips, and techniques for fishing after dark.
intro: As the sun goes down, many anglers pack up and head home. But for those who stay, some of the best fishing of the day is just beginning. Night fishing isn't just about escaping the summer heat — many species feed more actively after dark, and the reduced fishing pressure means bigger, less cautious fish.

title: Ontario Fish Species: Complete Guide to Native and Introduced Species
description: A comprehensive guide to the fish species found in Ontario's lakes and rivers, including native species, introduced species, and where to find them.
intro: Ontario is home to more than 150 species of freshwater fish — more than any other province in Canada. From the massive lake sturgeon to the tiny brook stickleback, Ontario's waters hold incredible diversity. Here's a guide to the most notable species.

title: Spinnerbait vs Crankbait vs Jerkbait: When to Use Each Lure
description: A comprehensive comparison of three essential bass lures — spinnerbaits, crankbaits, and jerkbaits — and when to use each one for best results.
intro: Spinnerbaits, crankbaits, and jerkbaits are three of the most versatile and effective bass lures ever designed. But knowing when to use which one can be the difference between a good day and a great day.

title: Top 10 Lures Every Walleye Angler Needs
description: The essential walleye lures that every angler should have in their tackle box, from jigs and crankbaits to spinner rigs and live bait setups.
intro: Walleye can be finicky, but having the right lures in your box means you're ready for whatever conditions you encounter. Here are the ten essential walleye lures every angler needs.

title: Top 10 Bass Fishing Lakes in Ontario
description: Discover the top 10 bass fishing lakes in Ontario, from Lake St. Clair to the Bay of Quinte. Species, tips, and best times to fish each location.
intro: Ontario holds more than 250,000 lakes — more freshwater than any other province or state in North America. For bass anglers, that means endless opportunities to find trophy smallmouth and largemouth. Whether you're a weekend warrior or a tournament competitor, these ten lakes deliver consistent action and unforgettable days on the water.

title: Top 10 Walleye Fishing Spots in Ontario
description: Discover the top walleye fishing locations in Ontario, from Lake Erie to Lake of the Woods. Tips on seasons, techniques, and what makes each lake special.
intro: Ontario holds more walleye than any other province or state. With thousands of lakes and rivers, finding good walleye water is easy — finding great walleye water takes a guide. Here are ten of the best walleye fisheries in Ontario.

title: Understanding Fish Behavior: How Fish Think, See & Feed
description: Learn how fish perceive their world — vision, hearing, smell, feeding triggers, and how water temperature and weather affect fish behavior.
intro: Every angler has asked the same question: "Why are they biting today and not yesterday?" The answer lies in understanding how fish perceive their environment. Fish aren't random — they respond to specific stimuli based on their senses, their biology, and the conditions around them.
